title: He Made $900 IN A DAY With A Faceless YouTube Channel - HOW?

transcript.text: Before I joined Order of YouTube, I was doing commentary. I was doing good money and I thought that it would continue for the rest of my life, basically, because I was an idiot. How easy was it for you to hire animators? Maybe you can even like screen share and show us some results. What do you think is the reason why at the beginning the videos didn't work? Can you show the the entire like channel history, like how how it blew up? And this was actually the first day of monetization. How big is the percentage of work that Claude is now doing for you? Right, everybody, I'm here with Ollie. Ollie, welcome to the pod. Ollie is a student of the Order of YouTube program and today we're talking about his journey because over the last months Ollie generated almost 100 million views, 80 plus million views, and on a pretty fresh channel because the channel is about 3 months old. About 2 months ago we started getting views and now the channel is up and running and scaling up like crazy. It was actually in the biker niche, I can reveal that, so it was like on motorbikes and everything. And like that was going good, but like as with commentary channels they die out, so I was doing good money and I thought that it would continue for the rest of my life, basically, because I was an idiot. Then it kind of went downhill a lot, even though I was posting more. And I I'm like the type of person I am, I like to build like one thing and like make it good, so I didn't really think that like commentary and like low effort niches suited me. So, when I saw like your program on Twitter, like I got really excited and wanted to build something more branded that like I could actually like feel good about [music] building. So, yeah, I wanted to build something stable and more branded and something to feel more proud of, basically. That's very interesting because not many people actually think about loving what they do when it comes to YouTube. I feel like the game became more like money, money, money, but at the end of the day like money is one thing, but loving what you do is another. Cool, interesting. And I I don't remember exactly how it happened, but I think when you reached out you didn't have any idea of the niche or anything, you just wanted to do branded. So, can you walk me through like your thought process at the beginning of this channel? Like what was very important to you, what were you trying to figure out and like how did that initial [music] step look like? Yeah, so as you said, I didn't really have an idea of what I wanted to do, but since I had experience with shorts, I knew that I wanted to do shorts, at least. So, I basically just scrolled on incognito for like maybe like 2 hours and I found like what what was it? Maybe like 20 niches and you helped me go through through every niche and the niche we decided to go into was also was also like the one that felt the most exciting to me because the videos had like a humorous style which felt like kind of fun to make. So yeah, and the channel was performing [music] good, of course, like the channel I took the inspiration from and that's really important. Like you can't just do something you feel good about, it [music] has to be like a proven niche. Combination of your interest and the demand on YouTube for for this kind of content. So, that was another thing. So, like at one point the channel had two strikes, so it was just one strike from like getting completely terminated. So, that was [music] also one of the reasons I wanted to switch. Yeah, makes sense. Like more like a creator, more like a more like a side hustle, it felt like more like a side hustle. But with this channel like when you have a team, when you like when you're paying out salaries, when you >> [music] >> like are tracking everything, it's it's way more business-like and it's not uh as you're not as much of a YouTuber anymore, you're more like a business owner. It was like a 2D video and that we recreated in 3D. I think that made the biggest difference, for sure. Right. And then there was a story, I think it's super funny. Yeah, it was it was stuck at like 60 views, like it was stuck. Yeah.
